2 [818087][ROSSINI] – Part B 1. EXCELLENCE 1.1 Objectives Our project, ROSSINI, aims to design, develop and demonstrate a modular and scalable platform for the integration of human-centred robotic technologies in industrial production environments. We will achieve this both by developing innovative technological components and methodologies in all fields related to collaborative robotics (sensing, control, actuation, human aspects, risk assessment methodology), and by integrating all such components in an open platform ensuring quick ramp-up and easy integration. Ultimately, we aim at making HRC a viable choice for manufacturers who up to now did not implement it, due to the regulatory and technological limitation of the technology. Indeed, by increasing the sensing capability, the intelligence, the fast reaction capacity and the communication skills of robots, we will deliver high performance, in terms of payloads, working speed and productivity, to HRC, thus ensuring that manufacturers enjoy the “best of both worlds” of industrial and collaborative robots. Our consortium, led by two large industrial stakeholders with a worldwide reputation as technological leaders in the field of safety in automation (PILZ and DATALOGIC), is inspired by the ambition to enable intelligent, dynamic HRC applications, in which task allocation, safety functions and working parameters can be easily adapted to production requirements, and to specific human operators’ needs. As such, our technological developments will build upon our current technologies (sensors, controllers and other mechatronic devices), which are already widely applied in robotics, and will achieve both components performance improvements and their structuring in a coherent and synergic platform. Moreover, through ROSSINI, we intend to develop and demonstrate a novel concept of “collaborative by birth” robotic arm, specifically designed for collaborating with humans, featuring built-in collaborative features for reducing reaction time and safe position signalling. As such, our platform will guarantee inherent safety to robotic applications developed through it, since all its components have been designed to maximise compliance with existing requirements for human-robot collaboration. By making safety an underlying, more easily fulfilled requirement, it will be then easier, for us as integrators and manufacturers, to shape HRC applications which maximise both productivity and job quality, in order to “optimally combine the repetitive performance of robots with the individual skills and ability of people” (ISO, 2015). To achieve this target, we shaped the ROSSINI consortium, aggregating European excellent research centres (UNIMORE, SUPSI, TNO and FRAUNHOFER), highly specialised innovative SMEs (IRIS, CRIT, CORE, MACHINEBOUW), and manufacturing end-users (IMA, SCHINDLER and WHIRLPOOL), which have agreed on a set of clear, measurable, realistic and achievable objectives. Specific Objective 1 - To design a Smart and Safe Sensing System with improved detection and tracking capabilities for monitoring the working environment, and a safety-graded fusion module for the processing of data Our platform shall include sensing technologies which radically improve the performance of currently available equipment, since closer human-robot collaboration first needs to rely on a highly detailed information base on the working environment. The ROSSINI Smart and Safe Sensing System (RS4) will pursue this goal, by combining information from several different customised sensing technologies (Vision, Laser Scanning, Radar, Mat, etc.) in order to track not only the position but also the speed of each operator and object in the scene. Moreover, the miscellaneous set of information generated by our platform sensing layer needs to be integrated in a single multidimensional image processed in real time to generate suitable 3D safety regions, or “dynamic safety shells”, around each object in the environment. This will be achieved by developing a dedicated RS4 fusion module, made of safety fieldbus communication and safety sensor modules controller. Means of verification. RS4, developed by DATALOGIC, will be validated by experienced PILZ risk assessment specialists using Collaborative Operations Method c) according to ISO/TS 150661. by Month 30, and is expected to show a 70% faster Response Time compared to currently available technologies. 1 ISO/TS 15066:2016 specifies safety requirements for collaborative industrial robot systems and the work environment, and supplements the requirements and guidance on collaborative industrial robot operation given in ISO 10218‑1 and ISO 10218‑2. 3 [818087][ROSSINI] – Part B Exploitation vision. The ROSSINI Smart and Safe Sensing system will be marketed by PILZ and DATALOGIC, building on our current companies’ technological offer, which already includes successful sensors for HRC such as the Sentinel Safety Laser Scanner and the A-, P-, T- Series Smart Cameras (from DATALOGIC), and the PILZ Safety Eye and Safety Mat. Specific Objective 2 - To develop a Safety Aware Control Architecture for robot cognitive perception and optimal task planning and execution In order to enable not only robot sensing, but actual perception, suitable data processing techniques based on artificial intelligence need to be deployed, such as advanced GPU computing and machine learning algorithms for image recognition, to obtain a semantic scene map that adapts to dynamic working conditions. This will allow us to add semantic information to simple geometric maps that are currently used by fusing sensor data, thus making it possible for the robot not only to visualize images, but also to interpret them. Moreover, the deployment of artificial intelligence techniques for data processing will not be just aimed to enable robot perception, but will also make robot cognition possible. In other words, besides being able to interpret data, our Safety Aware Control Architecture will make it possible for robots to optimally schedule the tasks that they need to accomplish, and to optimise the trade-off between human operator safety and manufacturing productivity. Each action to be executed will be sent to a dynamic planner that will dynamically optimize its execution in terms of trajectory to follow and/or interactive behaviour to reproduce while considering the variable safety conditions in the working area. Means of verification. The ROSSINI Safety Aware Control Architecture will be tested in the ARSControl Laboratory of UNIMORE by experienced research staff by Month 30, and is expected to earn a reduction of up to 45% of the overall Task Execution Time, and by 5% of the production reconfiguration time and cost. Exploitation Vision. The ROSSINI Safety Aware Control Architecture will represent a substantial evolution of our current PILZ motion control technology, now represented by the PRIMO motion and safety controller. Specific Objective 3 - To develop a “Collaborative by Birth” Robotic Arm range with novel built-in safety features Presently available collaborative robots are mainly standard robotic manipulators, on top of which specific technologies have been added in order to reduce risks for humans within collaborative operations. However, no substantial leap towards true Human Robot Collaboration can be obtained, without redesigning a robotic technology from scratch, considering safe interaction with humans as the main guiding principle at each design step. Through ROSSINI, we will work at the development of a new range of robotic manipulators, which will be offered to integrators as the platform actuation layer. The ROSSINI Natively Collaborative Robotic Arm will feature safe axis position signalling via double communication channels, and an innovative solution for coupling brushless motors within robot joints, thus speeding up the robot braking and retraction. Therefore, ROSSINI will introduce a new player in the market of robotic arms producers, a player which will take advantage of a wide experience, gained working directly on the shop floor assisting manufacturing companies for safe automation, and which will leverage on an established market reputation as “ambassadors of safety”. Means of verification. The ROSSINI “Collaborative by Birth” Robotic Arm will be validated by experienced PILZ risk assessment specialists by Month 28 with the support of the 3 industrial end users, and is expected to show a 45% reduction in the Protective Separation Distance within Collaborative Operations Method c) according to ISO/TS 15066. Exploitation Vision. The ROSSINI Collaborative by Birth Robotic Arm will be marketed by PILZ, and will be offered to customers that want to experiment the optimal synergy of all platform components into one specifically designed new concept of robotic manipulator. Specific Objective 4 – To develop a framework for Human-Robot Mutual Understanding in collaborative operations Our platform will incorporate a human-centred process design level to address and account for human factors like job quality, user experience, trust, feeling of safety, and liability, in the early design stages. Moreover, an adaptive level will constantly monitor the process progression, human behaviour, and robot behaviour. Dynamic allocation of tasks allows that online changes to the original task planning (as defined in 4 [818087][ROSSINI] – Part B the design level) can be made during operation. Finally, a profound mutual understanding between robots and people in operation will be realized (Communication Level) and thereby making human-robots collaborations more predictable. Means of verification. The ROSSINI Human-Robot Mutual Understanding Layer will be tested by Month 24 by TNO experts, and is expected to result in a 15% improvement of job quality, 25% increase in trust and feeling of safety, and 5% increase in system flexibility. Exploitation Vision. The ROSSINI Human-Robot Mutual Understanding level will result in a set of software tools to be integrated into robotic applications, and will be offered to robot integrators and manufacturers as a platform component, primarily through our consortium partners which are already performing robot integration (IMA, MACHINEBOUW). Specific Objective 5 – To integrate all the ROSSINI technological components into one inherently safe platform for HRC applications development. The innovative technologies made available through our research activities need to be made available to integrators and manufacturers in a complete package, which will ease the job of deploying human-robot collaboration on the shop floor
